description: 0.065% defective - Extremely strict quality level. Used for critical safety components and high-precision items.
0.10% defective - Very strict quality level. Used for safety-critical and high-reliability components.
0.15% defective - Very strict quality level. Used for critical components with zero-defect requirements.
0.25% defective - Strict quality level. Used for high-quality consumer products and critical components.
0.40% defective - Strict quality level. Used for premium quality products and important components.
0.65% defective - Strict quality level. Used for high-quality products where defects are costly.
1.0% defective - Standard quality level. Most commonly used for general manufacturing and consumer products.
1.5% defective - Standard quality level. Used for routine manufacturing processes and standard products.
2.5% defective - Standard quality level. Commonly used for general manufacturing and moderate quality requirements.
4.0% defective - Lenient quality level. Used for non-critical components and cost-sensitive products.
6.5% defective - Lenient quality level. Used for basic products where some defects are acceptable.
10% defective - Very lenient quality level. Used for low-cost items and non-critical applications.
15% defective - Very lenient quality level. Used for rough materials and preliminary inspections.
25% defective - Extremely lenient quality level. Used for raw materials and very basic quality checks.
40% defective - Extremely lenient quality level. Used for preliminary sorting and very rough inspections.
65% defective - Maximum lenient quality level. Used for initial material screening and rough sorting.
100% defective - Absolute maximum quality level. Used for material classification and gross defect screening.
150% defective - Ultimate quality level. Used only for special applications and initial material evaluation.
